prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion model for stock prices. We assume an annual drift of 8% (reflecting long-term market returns) and an annual volatility of 20% for ZTS. These parameters are annualized to daily figures and then scaled for the 18-day prediction period. The point estimate incorporates the assumed positive drift. Confidence intervals are calculated using the standard deviations of log-returns over the 18-day period, assuming a log-normal distribution for the stock price. The z-scores corresponding to the respective confidence levels (0.674 for 50%, 1.282 for 80%, 1.645 for 90%) are used to define the interval bounds.
